text: Mobile app development has become a thriving field in today's digital world. One crucial aspect is responsive design, which ensures that apps look and work well on different screen sizes, from phones to tablets. Developers often choose between building native apps, created specifically for iOS or Android using their core languages, or cross-platform apps, which use a single codebase that works on both systems. After development, apps are submitted to official app stores, like the Apple App Store or Google Play, where they must pass review processes. Ultimately, success depends on performance—apps must load quickly, run smoothly, and use device resources efficiently to retain users and receive positive reviews.
